text.title: Kubernetes Rolling Update Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules plus a skip-clause for `Kubernetes Rolling Update`. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: maxUnavailable MUST be at most 25 percent (default) or fewer; setting 0 is allowed only when maxSurge is at least 1.
rule.rationale: Without bound a rolling update can drop all replicas; pair with PDB.

rule.statement: Production Deployments MUST be paired with a PodDisruptionBudget setting minAvailable or maxUnavailable.
rule.rationale: Without PDB, voluntary disruptions (node drain) can take down all replicas at once.

rule.statement: minReadySeconds MUST be at least 10 for production services so a misbehaving pods readiness flap is filtered out.
rule.rationale: Without minReadySeconds a pod can flap ready to not-ready and look healthy.

rule.statement: progressDeadlineSeconds MUST be set (at most 600s); without deadline, stuck rollouts hide forever.
rule.rationale: Stuck rollouts without deadline never trigger automated rollback.

rule.statement: Production Deployments MUST use strategy.type RollingUpdate; Recreate is forbidden in production.
rule.rationale: Recreate brings down all replicas before new ones start; user-visible downtime.

rule.statement: If Applies If preconditions fail OR any Skip If trigger fires, do not run this methodology.
